calendar.description: Senate Judiciary, Law Enforcement, and Criminal Justice Hearing

general_provisions: This bill addresses provisions related to offenders.
hilighted_provisions: This bill:<hr><ltbullet>allows a court to order the Division of Adult Probation and Parole to supervise an individual convicted of class B misdemeanors under certain circumstances;<hr><ltbullet>requires the Board of Pardons and Parole (board), if considering whether to parole an offender who has previously been paroled and had the offender's parole revoked due to a new criminal offense, to consider the facts and circumstances of the new criminal offense in determining whether the offender should be paroled again;<hr><ltbullet>allows the board, with certain exceptions, to only consider a pardon for an offender who has committed an offense that requires the offender to register on the Sex, Kidnap, and Child Abuse Offender Registry (the registry) if:<hr><ltbullet1>for a sex offender required to register on the registry for 10 years, 10 years have passed from when the sex offender entered the community after the sex offender's conviction; or<hr><ltbullet1>for a sex offender required to register on the registry for the sex offender's lifetime, 20 years have passed from when the sex offender entered the community after the sex offender's conviction;<hr><ltbullet>does not allow an offender who has committed an offense that requires the offender to register on the registry at the time the offender is sentenced to receive a certificate of eligibility of expungement from the Bureau of Criminal Identification; and<hr><ltbullet>adds to the requirements for the board to meet before the board may parole an offender before the offender's minimum term of imprisonment has been met.
